description: Acute moist dermatitis (hot spot) on the neck of a Golden Retriever, red and oozing
notes: Hot spots are 3x more common in Golden Retrievers. Must identify underlying cause (allergy, flea, moisture). Not an emergency but needs treatment to prevent spread.

description: Circular lesion with hair loss on a Pit Bull puppy, scaly center
notes: Circular alopecia in puppy: rule out ringworm (zoonotic risk to family) and demodectic mange. Pit Bull puppies have elevated demodex susceptibility.

description: Rapidly growing firm mass on the forelimb of a Boxer
notes: Boxers have the HIGHEST breed risk for mast cell tumors. Any new skin mass in a Boxer must be evaluated with FNA/cytology. Never assume benign without pathological confirmation.

description: Normal healthy coat with no visible abnormalities in a Poodle
notes: Normal coat and skin. Must still ask about scratching, licking, or chewing behaviors that may not be visible in a single image.

description: Raised wheals (hives) across the body of a Bulldog, facial swelling
notes: Hives + facial swelling = allergic reaction. In brachycephalic breeds, facial swelling can compromise the airway. Must assess breathing quality. Risk of anaphylactic progression.

description: Deep infected wound with green discharge on the hind leg of a German Shepherd
notes: Green discharge = active bacterial infection. Must check for foreign body, assess depth, and evaluate for systemic infection signs (fever, lethargy). May need antibiotics and debridement.
